properties.displayName: UniFi Site Manager (CCF)

connectorUiConfig.title: UniFi Site Manager (CCF)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown:
Connects to the [UniFi Site Manager API](https://developer.ui.com/site-manager-api/gettingstarted) and ingests four data types: Sites, Devices, Hosts, and ISP Metrics.

**Prerequisites:**
1. Sign in to [UniFi Site Manager](https://unifi.ui.com)
2. Navigate to **API** in the left navigation
3. Click **Create API Key**
4. Copy and securely store the generated key (shown only once)

sampleQueries.query:
Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
| project TimeGenerated, siteId, hostId, permission, isOwner
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
| extend 
    siteName = tostring(meta.name),
    siteDescription = tostring(meta['desc']),
    timezone = tostring(meta.timezone),
    gatewayMac = tostring(meta.gatewayMac)
| project TimeGenerated, siteId, siteName, siteDescription, timezone, gatewayMac
| sort by siteName asc

Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
| summarize SiteCount = dcount(siteId) by permission
| sort by SiteCount desc

Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
| summarize 
    OwnedSites = dcountif(siteId, isOwner == true),
    SharedSites = dcountif(siteId, isOwner == false)

Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
| summarize SiteCount = dcount(siteId) by hostId
| sort by SiteCount desc

Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
| extend 
    siteName = tostring(meta.name),
    totalDevices = toint(siteStatistics.counts.totalDevice),
    onlineDevices = toint(siteStatistics.counts.onlineDevice),
    offlineDevices = toint(siteStatistics.counts.offlineDevice)
| project TimeGenerated, siteName, totalDevices, onlineDevices, offlineDevices
| sort by totalDevices desc

Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
| extend 
    siteName = tostring(meta.name),
    wiredClients = toint(siteStatistics.counts.wiredClient),
    wifiClients = toint(siteStatistics.counts.wifiClient),
    guestClients = toint(siteStatistics.counts.guestClient)
| project TimeGenerated, siteName, wiredClients, wifiClients, guestClients
| sort by (wiredClients + wifiClients) desc

Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
| extend timezone = tostring(meta.timezone)
| summarize SiteCount = dcount(siteId) by timezone
| sort by SiteCount desc

Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL
| project TimeGenerated, name, model, ip, mac, status, version, productLine
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL
| where status != "online"
| project TimeGenerated, name, model, ip, status, productLine
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL
| where isnotempty(updateAvailable)
| project TimeGenerated, name, model, version, updateAvailable, firmwareStatus
| sort by name asc

Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL
| summarize DeviceCount = dcount(id) by productLine
| sort by DeviceCount desc

Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL
| where isnotempty(adoptionTime)
| extend AdoptionDate = todatetime(adoptionTime)
| where AdoptionDate > ago(7d)
| project TimeGenerated, name, model, ip, AdoptionDate
| sort by AdoptionDate desc

Unifi_Cloud_Hosts_CL
| project TimeGenerated, id, hostType, ipAddress, owner, isBlocked
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

Unifi_Cloud_Hosts_CL
| where isBlocked == true
| project TimeGenerated, id, hostType, ipAddress
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

Unifi_Cloud_Hosts_CL
| summarize HostCount = dcount(id) by hostType
| sort by HostCount desc

Unifi_Cloud_Hosts_CL
| where isnotempty(lastConnectionStateChange)
| extend ConnectionChange = todatetime(lastConnectionStateChange)
| where ConnectionChange > ago(24h)
| project TimeGenerated, id, hostType, ipAddress, ConnectionChange
| sort by ConnectionChange desc

Unifi_Cloud_Hosts_CL
| where isnotempty(latestBackupTime)
| extend BackupTime = todatetime(latestBackupTime)
| where BackupTime > ago(7d)
| project TimeGenerated, id, hostType, ipAddress, BackupTime
| sort by BackupTime desc

Unifi_Cloud_Hosts_CL
| extend Apps = userData.apps
| mv-expand Apps
| summarize HostCount = dcount(id) by tostring(Apps)
| sort by HostCount desc

Unifi_Cloud_ISPMetrics_CL
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

Unifi_Cloud_ISPMetrics_CL
| mv-expand period = periods
| extend 
    periodStart = todatetime(period.start),
    periodEnd = todatetime(period.end),
    avgLatency = todouble(period.latency.avg),
    packetLoss = todouble(period.packetLoss),
    downloadKbps = todouble(period.download),
    uploadKbps = todouble(period.upload),
    uptime = todouble(period.uptime)
| project TimeGenerated, hostId, siteId, periodStart, avgLatency, packetLoss, downloadKbps, uploadKbps, uptime
| sort by periodStart desc

Unifi_Cloud_ISPMetrics_CL
| mv-expand period = periods
| extend avgLatency = todouble(period.latency.avg)
| where avgLatency > 100
| project TimeGenerated, hostId, siteId, avgLatency
| sort by avgLatency desc

Unifi_Cloud_ISPMetrics_CL
| mv-expand period = periods
| extend packetLoss = todouble(period.packetLoss)
| where packetLoss > 0
| project TimeGenerated, hostId, siteId, packetLoss
| sort by packetLoss desc

Unifi_Cloud_ISPMetrics_CL
| mv-expand period = periods
| extend 
    avgLatency = todouble(period.latency.avg),
    downloadKbps = todouble(period.download),
    uploadKbps = todouble(period.upload)
| summarize 
    AvgLatency = avg(avgLatency),
    AvgDownloadMbps = avg(downloadKbps) / 1000,
    AvgUploadMbps = avg(uploadKbps) / 1000
    by siteId
| sort by AvgLatency desc

Unifi_Cloud_ISPMetrics_CL
| mv-expand period = periods
| extend uptime = todouble(period.uptime)
| summarize AvgUptime = avg(uptime), MinUptime = min(uptime) by hostId
| sort by AvgUptime asc

Unifi_Cloud_ISPMetrics_CL
| mv-expand period = periods
| extend 
    periodStart = todatetime(period.start),
    downloadMbps = todouble(period.download) / 1000,
    uploadMbps = todouble(period.upload) / 1000
| summarize AvgDownload = avg(downloadMbps), AvgUpload = avg(uploadMbps) by bin(periodStart, 1h)
| sort by periodStart asc

3. Click **Create API Key**

dataFlows.transformKql: source | extend TimeGenerated = now() | project-rename siteStatistics = statistics
source | extend TimeGenerated = now() | project-rename hostType = type

columns.name: isConsole
columns.description: Whether device is a UniFi console

columns.name: hardwareId
columns.description: Hardware identifier of the host device

columns.name: hostType
columns.description: Type of host (e.g., ucore, uck, udm)

columns.name: owner
columns.description: Whether the API caller owns this host

columns.name: userData
columns.description: User-defined data including apps and console group members

columns.name: reportedState
columns.description: Host reported state (varies by UniFi OS version)

columns.name: metricType
columns.description: Type of ISP metric (e.g., latency, speed, uptime)

columns.name: hostId
columns.description: Unique identifier of the host reporting metrics

columns.name: periods
columns.description: Array of metric periods containing ISP performance data

columns.name: hostId
columns.description: Unique identifier of the host managing this site

columns.name: meta
columns.description: Site metadata including name, description, timezone, and gateway MAC

columns.name: siteStatistics
columns.description: Site statistics including device counts, client counts, and performance metrics

columns.name: permission
columns.description: API caller's permission level for this site

columns.name: isOwner
columns.description: Whether the API caller owns this site

instructionSteps.title: 1. Obtain a UniFi Site Manager API Key
instructionSteps.description:
1. Sign in to [UniFi Site Manager](https://unifi.ui.com)
2. Click **API** in the left navigation
3. Click **Create API Key**
4. Copy the generated key immediately - it will only be displayed once

instructionSteps.title: 2. Connect
instructionSteps.description: Enter your API key below. This single key authorizes polling for all four data types (Sites, Devices, Hosts, ISP Metrics).

parameters.label: UniFi Site Manager API Key
parameters.placeholder: Enter your API key from unifi.ui.com
parameters.name: apiKey

properties.displayName: UniFi Site Manager (CCF)

properties.description: Identifies when a UniFi controller (Cloud Key, Dream Machine, etc.) experiences a connection state change, which may indicate a network outage, power loss, or device failure.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Controller Connection State Change
properties.query:
// UniFi Controller Connection State Change Detection
// Author: Fetch Labs (https://sentinel.blog)
Unifi_Cloud_Hosts_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where isnotempty(lastConnectionStateChange)
| extend ConnectionChange = todatetime(lastConnectionStateChange)
| where ConnectionChange > ago(1h)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by id
| extend 
    HostTypeDisplay = case(
        hostType == "ucore", "UniFi OS Console",
        hostType == "uck", "Cloud Key",
        hostType == "uckp", "Cloud Key+",
        hostType == "udm", "Dream Machine",
        hostType == "udmpro", "Dream Machine Pro",
        hostType == "udmse", "Dream Machine SE",
        hostType == "self-hosted", "Self-Hosted",
        hostType
    )
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    ConnectionChangeTime = ConnectionChange,
    HostId = id,
    HostType = hostType,
    HostTypeDisplay,
    IPAddress = ipAddress,
    IsBlocked = isBlocked,
    IsOwner = owner

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Controller Connection State Change

properties.description: Identifies when UniFi Site Manager data connectors stop sending data, ensuring visibility into the UniFi estate is maintained.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Data Connector Health
properties.query:
// UniFi Data Connector Health Monitoring
// Author: Fetch Labs (https://sentinel.blog)
let StaleThreshold = 30;
let DevicesHealth = Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
| summarize LastReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
| extend TableName = "Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL", TableDisplay = "Devices";
let HostsHealth = Unifi_Cloud_Hosts_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
| summarize LastReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
| extend TableName = "Unifi_Cloud_Hosts_CL", TableDisplay = "Hosts";
let SitesHealth = Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
| summarize LastReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
| extend TableName = "Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL", TableDisplay = "Sites";
let ISPHealth = Unifi_Cloud_ISPMetrics_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
| summarize LastReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
| extend TableName = "Unifi_Cloud_ISPMetrics_CL", TableDisplay = "ISP Metrics";
union DevicesHealth, HostsHealth, SitesHealth, ISPHealth
| extend 
    MinutesSinceData = datetime_diff('minute', now(), LastReceived),
    Status = case(
        datetime_diff('minute', now(), LastReceived) <= 15, "Healthy",
        datetime_diff('minute', now(), LastReceived) <= 30, "Delayed",
        datetime_diff('minute', now(), LastReceived) <= 60, "Stale",
        "Critical"
    )
| where MinutesSinceData >= StaleThreshold
| extend TimeGenerated = now()
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    TableName,
    TableDisplay,
    LastReceived,
    MinutesSinceData,
    Status

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Data Connector Health

properties.description: UniFiCloudDeviceOffline_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.3
Identifies when a UniFi device transitions to offline status, which may indicate a network outage, power loss, or hardware failure requiring attention.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Device Offline
properties.query:
// UniFi Device Offline Detection
// Author: Fetch Labs (https://sentinel.blog)
Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by id
| where status == "offline"
| extend OfflineMinutes = datetime_diff('minute', now(), TimeGenerated)
| where OfflineMinutes >= 10
| extend
    DeviceName = coalesce(name, "Unnamed"),
    DeviceId = id,
    Model = model,
    IPAddress = ip,
    MACAddress = mac,
    ProductLine = productLine,
    FirmwareVersion = version
| extend hostName = DeviceName
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    DeviceName,
    DeviceId,
    Model,
    IPAddress,
    MACAddress,
    ProductLine,
    Status = status,
    OfflineMinutes,
    FirmwareVersion,
    hostName

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Device Offline

properties.description: Identifies when a site reports more than one distinct WAN external IP within an hour, which may indicate ISP DHCP refresh, WAN reconfiguration, or routing hijack.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: External WAN IP changed
properties.query:
Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
      | where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
      | extend siteName = tostring(meta.name),
               currentIp = tostring(siteStatistics.wans.WAN.externalIp)
      | where isnotempty(currentIp)
      | summarize DistinctIps = make_set(currentIp), arg_max(TimeGenerated, currentIp) by siteId, siteName
      | where array_length(DistinctIps) > 1
      | extend Activity = strcat('WAN IP changed; observed: ', tostring(DistinctIps))
      | project TimeGenerated, siteId, siteName, Activity, CurrentIp = currentIp, ObservedIps = DistinctIps

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: External WAN IP changed

properties.description: Identifies UniFi devices with firmware updates available. Keeping firmware patched is critical as updates often include security vulnerability fixes.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Firmware Update Available
properties.query:
// UniFi Firmware Update Available Detection
// Author: Fetch Labs (https://sentinel.blog)
let DeviceThreshold = 1;
Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by id
| where isnotempty(updateAvailable)
| summarize 
    DevicesNeedingUpdate = count(),
    DeviceList = make_list(pack(
        "name", coalesce(name, "Unnamed"),
        "model", model,
        "currentVersion", version,
        "availableVersion", updateAvailable,
        "ip", ip
    )),
    Models = make_set(model),
    ProductLines = make_set(productLine)
| where DevicesNeedingUpdate >= DeviceThreshold
| extend 
    TimeGenerated = now(),
    AffectedModels = strcat_array(Models, ", "),
    AffectedProductLines = strcat_array(ProductLines, ", ")
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    DevicesNeedingUpdate,
    AffectedModels,
    AffectedProductLines

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Firmware Update Available

properties.description: Identifies when a UniFi gateway threat-protection state transitions away from the expected IPS-active mode, which may indicate admin action or attacker tampering.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: IPS/IDS disabled or misconfigured
properties.query:
let prev = Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
          | where TimeGenerated between (ago(30m) .. ago(15m))
          | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by tostring(siteId)
          | project siteId_s = tostring(siteId),
                    prevIpsMode = tostring(siteStatistics.gateway.ipsMode),
                    prevInspection = tostring(siteStatistics.gateway.inspectionState);
      Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
      | where TimeGenerated > ago(15m)
      | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by tostring(siteId)
      | extend siteId_s = tostring(siteId),
               siteName = tostring(meta.name),
               ipsMode = tostring(siteStatistics.gateway.ipsMode),
               inspectionState = tostring(siteStatistics.gateway.inspectionState)
      | join kind=inner prev on siteId_s
      | where (ipsMode != 'ips' and prevIpsMode == 'ips')
           or (inspectionState != 'on' and prevInspection == 'on')
      | extend Activity = strcat('Threat protection degraded - ipsMode: ', prevIpsMode, ' -> ', ipsMode, ', inspection: ', prevInspection, ' -> ', inspectionState)
      | project TimeGenerated, siteId, siteName, Activity, prevIpsMode, ipsMode, prevInspection, inspectionState

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: IPS/IDS disabled or misconfigured

properties.description: Identifies when the IPS signature ruleset count drops by more than half versus the 7-day maximum, indicating broken threat-intel feeds or rollback.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: IPS signature count dropped >50%
properties.query:
Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
      | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
      | extend siteName = tostring(meta.name),
               rulesCount = toint(siteStatistics.gateway.ipsSignature.rulesCount)
      | where isnotnull(rulesCount)
      | summarize MaxCount = max(rulesCount), arg_max(TimeGenerated, rulesCount) by siteId, siteName
      | where MaxCount > 100 and toreal(rulesCount) / MaxCount < 0.5
      | extend DropPct = round(100.0 * (1 - toreal(rulesCount) / MaxCount), 1)
      | extend Activity = strcat('IPS rules dropped to ', rulesCount, ' (from ', MaxCount, ', -', DropPct, '%)')
      | project TimeGenerated, siteId, siteName, Activity, CurrentRules = rulesCount, PeakRules = MaxCount, DropPct

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: IPS signature count dropped >50%

properties.description: UniFiCloudISPDowntime_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.3
Identifies when the ISP connection experiences downtime, impacting business operations and requiring documentation for SLA purposes.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: ISP Downtime
properties.query:
// UniFi ISP Downtime Detection
// Author: Fetch Labs (https://sentinel.blog)
Unifi_Cloud_ISPMetrics_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30m)
| mv-expand period = periods
| extend 
    metricTime = todatetime(period.metricTime),
    downtime = toint(period.data.wan.downtime),
    uptime = todouble(period.data.wan.uptime),
    ispName = tostring(period.data.wan.ispName),
    ispAsn = tostring(period.data.wan.ispAsn)
| where downtime > 0
| summarize 
    TotalDowntimeSeconds = sum(downtime),
    EventCount = count(),
    AvgUptime = round(avg(uptime), 2),
    FirstSeen = min(metricTime),
    LastSeen = max(metricTime)
    by siteId, ispName, ispAsn
| extend 
    TimeGenerated = now(),
    DowntimeMinutes = round(TotalDowntimeSeconds / 60.0, 2)
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    SiteId = siteId,
    ISPName = ispName,
    ISPAsn = ispAsn,
    TotalDowntimeSeconds,
    DowntimeMinutes,
    AvgUptimePct = AvgUptime,
    EventCount,
    FirstSeen,
    LastSeen

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: ISP Downtime

properties.description: UniFiCloudISPHighLatency_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.3
Identifies when ISP latency exceeds acceptable thresholds, impacting VoIP quality, video conferencing, and overall application performance.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: ISP High Latency
properties.query:
// UniFi ISP High Latency Detection
// Author: Fetch Labs (https://sentinel.blog)
let AvgLatencyThreshold = 100;
let MaxLatencyThreshold = 500;
Unifi_Cloud_ISPMetrics_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30m)
| mv-expand period = periods
| extend 
    metricTime = todatetime(period.metricTime),
    avgLatency = toint(period.data.wan.avgLatency),
    maxLatency = toint(period.data.wan.maxLatency),
    ispName = tostring(period.data.wan.ispName),
    ispAsn = tostring(period.data.wan.ispAsn)
| where avgLatency > AvgLatencyThreshold or maxLatency > MaxLatencyThreshold
| summarize 
    EventCount = count(),
    AvgLatency = avg(avgLatency),
    MaxLatency = max(maxLatency),
    FirstSeen = min(metricTime),
    LastSeen = max(metricTime)
    by siteId, ispName, ispAsn
| extend 
    TimeGenerated = now(),
    AvgLatencyRounded = round(AvgLatency, 1)
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    SiteId = siteId,
    ISPName = ispName,
    ISPAsn = ispAsn,
    AvgLatencyMs = AvgLatencyRounded,
    MaxLatencyMs = MaxLatency,
    EventCount,
    FirstSeen,
    LastSeen

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: ISP High Latency

properties.description: UniFiCloudISPPacketLoss_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.3
Identifies when WAN packet loss occurs. Even small loss percentages can significantly degrade VoIP calls and video conferencing quality.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: ISP Packet Loss
properties.query:
// UniFi ISP Packet Loss Detection
// Author: Fetch Labs (https://sentinel.blog)
let PacketLossThreshold = 1;
Unifi_Cloud_ISPMetrics_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30m)
| mv-expand period = periods
| extend 
    metricTime = todatetime(period.metricTime),
    packetLoss = toint(period.data.wan.packetLoss),
    ispName = tostring(period.data.wan.ispName),
    ispAsn = tostring(period.data.wan.ispAsn),
    avgLatency = toint(period.data.wan.avgLatency)
| where packetLoss >= PacketLossThreshold
| summarize 
    TotalPacketLoss = sum(packetLoss),
    EventCount = count(),
    AvgLatency = avg(avgLatency),
    FirstSeen = min(metricTime),
    LastSeen = max(metricTime)
    by siteId, ispName, ispAsn
| extend TimeGenerated = now()
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    SiteId = siteId,
    ISPName = ispName,
    ISPAsn = ispAsn,
    TotalPacketLoss,
    EventCount,
    AvgLatencyMs = round(AvgLatency, 1),
    FirstSeen,
    LastSeen

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: ISP Packet Loss

properties.description: UniFiCloudISPSLABreach_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.3
Identifies when ISP uptime falls below the SLA threshold. Useful for tracking SLA compliance and supporting ISP accountability conversations.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: ISP SLA Breach
properties.query:
// UniFi ISP SLA Breach Detection
// Author: Fetch Labs (https://sentinel.blog)
let SLAThreshold = 99.9;
Unifi_Cloud_ISPMetrics_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| mv-expand period = periods
| extend 
    metricTime = todatetime(period.metricTime),
    uptime = todouble(period.data.wan.uptime),
    downtime = toint(period.data.wan.downtime),
    ispName = tostring(period.data.wan.ispName),
    ispAsn = tostring(period.data.wan.ispAsn)
| summarize 
    AvgUptime = round(avg(uptime), 3),
    MinUptime = round(min(uptime), 3),
    TotalDowntimeSeconds = sum(downtime),
    MeasurementCount = count()
    by siteId, ispName, ispAsn
| where AvgUptime < SLAThreshold
| extend 
    TimeGenerated = now(),
    SLATarget = SLAThreshold,
    UptimeGap = round(SLAThreshold - AvgUptime, 3)
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    SiteId = siteId,
    ISPName = ispName,
    ISPAsn = ispAsn,
    AvgUptimePct = AvgUptime,
    MinUptimePct = MinUptime,
    SLATargetPct = SLATarget,
    UptimeGapPct = UptimeGap,
    TotalDowntimeSeconds,
    MeasurementCount

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: ISP SLA Breach

properties.description: Identifies when multiple UniFi devices go offline simultaneously, typically signaling a power outage, upstream connectivity failure, or infrastructure issue requiring investigation.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Multiple Devices Offline
properties.query:
// UniFi Multiple Devices Offline Detection
// Author: Fetch Labs (https://sentinel.blog)
let OfflineThreshold = 3;
Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30m)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by id
| where status == "offline"
| summarize 
    OfflineDeviceCount = count(),
    OfflineDevices = make_list(coalesce(name, id)),
    OfflineModels = make_list(model),
    OfflineIPs = make_list(ip),
    ProductLines = make_set(productLine)
| where OfflineDeviceCount >= OfflineThreshold
| extend 
    TimeGenerated = now(),
    OfflineDeviceList = strcat_array(OfflineDevices, ", "),
    AffectedProductLines = strcat_array(ProductLines, ", ")
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    OfflineDeviceCount,
    OfflineDeviceList,
    AffectedProductLines

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Multiple Devices Offline

properties.description: Identifies when a new device is adopted into the UniFi network. While often legitimate, unexpected adoptions may indicate unauthorized hardware additions.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: New Device Adopted
properties.query:
// UniFi New Device Adopted Detection
// Author: Fetch Labs (https://sentinel.blog)
Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where isnotempty(adoptionTime)
| extend AdoptionDateTime = todatetime(adoptionTime)
| where AdoptionDateTime > ago(1h)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by id
| extend
    DeviceName = coalesce(name, "Unnamed"),
    DeviceId = id,
    Model = model,
    IPAddress = ip,
    MACAddress = mac,
    ProductLine = productLine,
    FirmwareVersion = version,
    Shortname = shortname
| extend hostName = DeviceName
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    AdoptionTime = AdoptionDateTime,
    DeviceName,
    DeviceId,
    Model,
    IPAddress,
    MACAddress,
    ProductLine,
    Status = status,
    FirmwareVersion,
    Shortname,
    hostName

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: New Device Adopted

properties.description: Identifies a new issue index on the secondary WAN failover link. Fires only on newly-recorded issue indexes, not while existing issues persist.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: New WAN2 (secondary) issue recorded
properties.query:
let prev = Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
          | where TimeGenerated between (ago(30m) .. ago(15m))
          | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by tostring(siteId)
          | mv-expand pi = todynamic(tostring(siteStatistics.wans.WAN2.wanIssues))
          | extend siteId_s = tostring(siteId), prevIdx = tolong(pi.index)
          | distinct siteId_s, prevIdx;
      Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
      | where TimeGenerated > ago(15m)
      | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by tostring(siteId)
      | where isnotempty(tostring(siteStatistics.wans.WAN2))
      | extend siteId_s = tostring(siteId), siteName = tostring(meta.name)
      | mv-expand issue = todynamic(tostring(siteStatistics.wans.WAN2.wanIssues))
      | extend idx = tolong(issue.index)
      | join kind=leftanti prev on $left.siteId_s == $right.siteId_s, $left.idx == $right.prevIdx
      | extend Activity = strcat('New WAN2 issue index ', idx, ' on secondary WAN (downtime=', tostring(issue.wanDowntime), ' highLatency=', tostring(issue.highLatency), ')')
      | project TimeGenerated, siteId, siteName, Activity, IssueIndex=idx, IssueDetail=issue

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: New WAN2 (secondary) issue recorded

properties.description: Identifies a newly-recorded UniFi WAN issue index. May indicate ISP problems, hardware fault, or active denial-of-service pressure on the gateway.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: New WAN issue index recorded
properties.query:
let prev = Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
          | where TimeGenerated between (ago(30m) .. ago(15m))
          | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by tostring(siteId)
          | mv-expand pi = todynamic(tostring(siteStatistics.wans.WAN.wanIssues))
          | extend siteId_s = tostring(siteId), prevIdx = tolong(pi.index)
          | distinct siteId_s, prevIdx;
      Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
      | where TimeGenerated > ago(15m)
      | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by tostring(siteId)
      | extend siteId_s = tostring(siteId),
               siteName = tostring(meta.name)
      | mv-expand issue = todynamic(tostring(siteStatistics.wans.WAN.wanIssues))
      | extend idx = tolong(issue.index)
      | join kind=leftanti prev on $left.siteId_s == $right.siteId_s, $left.idx == $right.prevIdx
      | extend Activity = strcat('New WAN issue index ', idx, ' on site (downtime=', tostring(issue.wanDowntime), ' highLatency=', tostring(issue.highLatency), ')'),
               peakMs = toint(issue.latencyMaxMs)
      | project TimeGenerated, siteId, siteName, Activity, IssueIndex=idx, PeakLatencyMs=peakMs, IssueDetail=issue

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: New WAN issue index recorded

properties.description: Identifies when the UniFi critical-notification count increases since the previous poll, signaling at least one new critical-severity alarm has appeared.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: New critical notifications appeared
properties.query:
let prev = Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
          | where TimeGenerated between (ago(30m) .. ago(15m))
          | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by tostring(siteId)
          | project siteId_s = tostring(siteId),
                    prevCritical = toint(siteStatistics.counts.criticalNotification);
      Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
      | where TimeGenerated > ago(15m)
      | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by tostring(siteId)
      | extend siteId_s = tostring(siteId),
               siteName = tostring(meta.name),
               CriticalCount = toint(siteStatistics.counts.criticalNotification)
      | join kind=inner prev on siteId_s
      | where CriticalCount > prevCritical
      | extend NewCriticals = CriticalCount - prevCritical
      | extend Activity = strcat(NewCriticals, ' new critical notification(s) - count went ', prevCritical, ' -> ', CriticalCount)
      | project TimeGenerated, siteId, siteName, Activity, prevCritical, CriticalCount, NewCriticals

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: New critical notifications appeared

properties.description: Identifies sites with pending firmware updates outstanding continuously for the last 7 days, indicating patch debt that should be scheduled.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Pending firmware updates outstanding for 7d+
properties.query:
Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
      | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
      | extend siteName = tostring(meta.name),
               pendingUpdates = toint(siteStatistics.counts.pendingUpdateDevice)
      | summarize MinPending = min(pendingUpdates), MaxPending = max(pendingUpdates), arg_max(TimeGenerated, pendingUpdates) by siteId, siteName
      | where MinPending > 0
      | extend Activity = strcat('Pending updates sustained - current ', pendingUpdates, ' device(s)')
      | project TimeGenerated, siteId, siteName, Activity, PendingDevices = pendingUpdates

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Pending firmware updates outstanding for 7d+

properties.description: Identifies when a site has multiple offline devices, indicating significant site-level issues such as a power outage, network failure, or infrastructure problem.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Site Health Critical
properties.query:
// UniFi Site Health Critical Detection
// Author: Fetch Labs (https://sentinel.blog)
let OfflineThreshold = 3;
Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30m)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by siteId
| extend 
    siteName = tostring(meta.name),
    timezone = tostring(meta.timezone),
    totalDevices = toint(siteStatistics.counts.totalDevice),
    offlineDevices = toint(siteStatistics.counts.offlineDevice),
    wifiClients = toint(siteStatistics.counts.wifiClient),
    wiredClients = toint(siteStatistics.counts.wiredClient)
| where offlineDevices >= OfflineThreshold
| extend 
    AvailabilityPct = round(100.0 * (totalDevices - offlineDevices) / totalDevices, 1),
    TotalClients = wifiClients + wiredClients
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    SiteId = siteId,
    SiteName = siteName,
    TotalDevices = totalDevices,
    OfflineDevices = offlineDevices,
    OnlineDevices = totalDevices - offlineDevices,
    AvailabilityPct,
    TotalClients,
    WiFiClients = wifiClients,
    WiredClients = wiredClients,
    Timezone = timezone

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Site Health Critical

properties.description: Identifies when a UniFi host transitions cloudSystemLogState from enabled to disabled. This defense-evasion signal may indicate an attacker silencing audit trails.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: System log shipping disabled
properties.query:
let prev = Unifi_Cloud_Hosts_CL
          | where TimeGenerated between (ago(30m) .. ago(15m))
          | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by id
          | project id_s = id, prevState = tostring(reportedState.cloudSystemLogState);
      Unifi_Cloud_Hosts_CL
      | where TimeGenerated > ago(15m)
      | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by id
      | extend id_s = id,
               siteName = tostring(reportedState.name),
               currentState = tostring(reportedState.cloudSystemLogState)
      | join kind=inner prev on id_s
      | where currentState == 'disabled' and prevState == 'enabled'
      | extend Activity = strcat('Cloud system log shipping was disabled on host ', siteName, ' (', prevState, ' -> ', currentState, ')')
      | project TimeGenerated, id, siteName, Activity, prevState, currentState

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: System log shipping disabled

properties.description: Identifies when site WAN uptime falls below 99 percent, an SLA-grade availability problem. Combine with the ISP Downtime rule for root-cause attribution.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: WAN uptime below 99%
properties.query:
Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
      | where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
      | extend siteName = tostring(meta.name),
               wanUptime = todouble(siteStatistics.percentages.wanUptime)
      | where isnotnull(wanUptime) and wanUptime < 99.0
      | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by siteId, siteName
      | extend Activity = strcat('WAN uptime ', round(wanUptime, 2), '% (below 99% SLA threshold)')
      | project TimeGenerated, siteId, siteName, Activity, WanUptimePct = round(wanUptime, 2)

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: WAN uptime below 99%

properties.description: Identifies when the site-wide WiFi TX retry rate exceeds 5 percent, which may indicate RF interference, channel congestion, or active wireless attack.
properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: WiFi quality degraded (high TX retry)
properties.query:
Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
      | where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
      | extend siteName = tostring(meta.name),
               txRetry = todouble(siteStatistics.percentages.txRetry)
      | where txRetry > 5.0
      | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by siteId, siteName
      | extend Activity = strcat('TX retry rate ', round(txRetry, 1), '% (>5% threshold)')
      | project TimeGenerated, siteId, siteName, Activity, TxRetryPct = round(txRetry, 1)

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: WiFi quality degraded (high TX retry)
UniFi Cloud: Console group membership churn
properties.query:
Unifi_Cloud_Hosts_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| mv-expand member = userData.consoleGroupMembers
| summarize MembersByDay = make_set(tostring(member.mac)) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d), id, HostName = tostring(reportedState.name)
| extend MemberCount = array_length(MembersByDay)
| order by id, TimeGenerated asc
| extend Prev = prev(MembersByDay), PrevId = prev(id)
| where id == PrevId and array_length(set_difference(MembersByDay, coalesce(Prev, dynamic([])))) > 0
| project Date=TimeGenerated, HostName, ['New members'] = set_difference(MembersByDay, coalesce(Prev, dynamic([]))), MemberCount

tags.name: description
tags.value: Tracks members of `userData.consoleGroupMembers` over time. Additions indicate new admin access; removals indicate revoked access. Both warrant review - additions are an account-creation signal, removals can be a sign of attacker cleanup.

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Console group membership churn

properties.description: UniFiCloudDeviceFlapping_HuntingQueries Hunting Query with template version 3.0.3

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Devices flapping online/offline
properties.query:
Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| extend StatusBin = bin(TimeGenerated, 30m)
| summarize Statuses = make_set(status) by id, name, model, productLine, StatusBin
| where array_length(Statuses) > 1
| summarize Flaps = count() by id, HostName = name, model, productLine
| where Flaps > 4
| order by Flaps desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Devices that transitioned between online and offline more than 4 times in 24 hours. Flapping can indicate intermittent connectivity, hardware failure, environmental issues, or active interference targeting the device.

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Devices flapping online/offline
UniFi Cloud: Firmware drift hotspots
properties.query:
Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by id
| where firmwareStatus in ("updateAvailable", "updatePending")
| summarize Devices=count(),
            SampleDevice = take_any(name),
            ['Pending updates'] = countif(firmwareStatus =~ "updatePending"),
            ['Updates available'] = countif(firmwareStatus =~ "updateAvailable")
            by Model = model, ['Product line'] = productLine
| order by Devices desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Devices behind on firmware updates - ranked by product line and model. Identifies models where firmware drift is concentrated, indicating either a vendor-side delay or operational neglect. Each unpatched device is a CVE attack surface.

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Firmware drift hotspots
UniFi Cloud: Firmware version diversity within a model
properties.query:
Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2h)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by id
| summarize Devices = count(),
            SampleDevice = take_any(name),
            ['Distinct versions'] = make_set(version),
            ['Up-to-date count'] = countif(firmwareStatus =~ "upToDate")
            by Model = model
| extend ['Version count'] = array_length(['Distinct versions'])
| where ['Version count'] > 1
| order by ['Version count'] desc, Devices desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Models running multiple distinct firmware versions across the estate. Indicates inconsistent patch management - some units are forgotten or excluded from update windows.

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Firmware version diversity within a model
UniFi Cloud: Long-tail ISP latency hotspots (P95)
properties.query:
Unifi_Cloud_ISPMetrics_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| mv-expand period = periods
| extend AvgMs = todouble(period.data.wan.avgLatency),
         MaxMs = todouble(period.data.wan.maxLatency)
| summarize ['Median ms'] = percentile(AvgMs, 50),
            ['P95 ms']    = percentile(AvgMs, 95),
            ['P99 ms']    = percentile(MaxMs, 99),
            Samples=count() by siteId = tostring(siteId)
| extend ['P95/median ratio'] = round(['P95 ms'] / ['Median ms'], 2)
| where ['P95/median ratio'] > 5
| order by ['P95/median ratio'] desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Sites where the P95 ISP latency is significantly above the median over the last 7 days. Helps identify sites suffering occasional but severe latency spikes that average-based monitoring misses.

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Long-tail ISP latency hotspots (P95)
UniFi Cloud: Devices adopted outside business hours
properties.query:
Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by id
| where isnotempty(adoptionTime)
| extend AdoptedAt = todatetime(adoptionTime),
         AdoptedHour = hourofday(todatetime(adoptionTime)),
         AdoptedDow = dayofweek(todatetime(adoptionTime))
| where (AdoptedHour < 6 or AdoptedHour >= 22) or AdoptedDow in (0d, 6d)
| project HostName=name, Model=model, ['Product line']=productLine, AdoptedAt, AdoptedHour, IPAddress=ip
| order by AdoptedAt desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: New devices adopted into a UniFi site outside business hours (10pm - 6am local time, or weekends). Legitimate admin work usually happens in hours; unattended adoption can be unauthorised hardware joining the network.

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Devices adopted outside business hours
UniFi Cloud: Sites with persistent WAN issues
properties.query:
Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| extend Site = tostring(meta.name),
         IssueCount = array_length(parse_json(tostring(siteStatistics.wans.WAN.wanIssues)))
| summarize TotalPolls = count(),
            PollsWithIssues = countif(IssueCount > 0) by HostName = Site
| extend ['Issue rate %'] = round(100.0 * PollsWithIssues / TotalPolls, 1)
| where ['Issue rate %'] > 50 and TotalPolls > 12
| order by ['Issue rate %'] desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Sites that have recorded WAN issues in the majority of polls over the last 24 hours. Indicates chronic ISP problems versus transient blips - escalate to provider.

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: Sites with persistent WAN issues
UniFi Cloud: WAN external IP geographic deviation
properties.query:
Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| extend Site = tostring(meta.name),
         WanIp = tostring(siteStatistics.wans.WAN.externalIp),
         Asn = toint(siteStatistics.ispInfo.asn),
         Isp = tostring(siteStatistics.ispInfo.name)
| where isnotempty(WanIp)
| summarize ['Distinct ASNs'] = make_set(Asn),
            ['Distinct ISPs'] = make_set(Isp),
            ['WAN IPs']      = make_set(WanIp),
            ['First seen']   = min(TimeGenerated),
            ['Last seen']    = max(TimeGenerated) by HostName = Site
| extend ['ASN count'] = array_length(['Distinct ASNs']),
         ['ISP count'] = array_length(['Distinct ISPs']),
         IPAddress = tostring(['WAN IPs'][0])
| where ['ASN count'] > 1 or ['ISP count'] > 1
| order by ['ASN count'] desc, ['ISP count'] desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Sites where the WAN external IP changed ASN or ISP within the last 30 days. Routine DHCP renewal stays within the same ISP; an ASN/ISP change suggests provider switch, BGP hijack, or routing anomaly that warrants verification.

properties.displayName: UniFi Cloud: WAN external IP geographic deviation

properties.displayName: [parameters('workbook1-name')]
UniFi Site Manager (CCF)
properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The UniFi Site Manager solution for Microsoft Sentinel provides cloud-side telemetry ingestion via the Site Manager API for sites, devices, hosts and ISP metrics. Ships analytics rules covering ISP downtime, WAN issues, IPS/IDS posture, firmware drift, device offline events, configuration changes and security signals, plus an operations workbook for at-a-glance estate health.

Data Connector: UniFi Site Manager (CCF) - single Connect deploys 4 polling rules with a single API key.

Underlying API tier: the Site Manager API is available on all UniFi cloud plans. The Audit log endpoint requires Pro+; this solution does not depend on it.

Pre-requisites: A UniFi Site Manager API key is required. Generate one at https://unifi.ui.com/api.

Data Connectors: 1, Workbooks: 1, Analytic Rules: 21, Hunting Queries: 8
